ground_truth.title: 7 SEO and AI Search Trends to Watch in 2026
ground_truth.author: Henry Purchase
ground_truth.main_content: With 68% of online experiences starting with a search, ranking high in search engine results helps to generate leads for your business or brand. As AI reshapes the way search results appear and how people discover information online, staying updated with recent trends is essential. I’m Henry Purchase, founder of SEOSpace, the SEO extension for Squarespace. In this article, I’ll cover seven key SEO and AI search trends for 2026, along with challenges and tips to stay ahead. If you’re new to SEO, start with our introductory guide. 1. AI-first search AI assistants like ChatGPT are transforming how people search, with AI expected to handle 25% of global queries by 2026. This shifts search results away from the traditional “10 blue links” model and toward direct answers that are personalized to a user’s search history, preferences, and past behavior. Once you try this yourself, it becomes clear why some people prefer AI-powered results. They can get the information they need instantly, instead of clicking through multiple websites. AI search also offers more straightforward responses—right now, users don’t have to navigate past ads, sidebars, or carousels to reach details or page links. Search engines like Google are also continuing to roll out more AI overviews in results to create a similar search experience, pushing individual website results further down the page. AI overviews AI overviews are becoming mainstream in search results. These are summaries that directly answer a user’s question at the top of the search engine results page (SERP), reducing the need to scroll through individual results. While the aim is to improve user experience, this trend is not without its drawbacks. Test it out yourself: Go to Google or another major search engine and search for “website builder” or “Squarespace SEO.” AI overviews currently appear above organic results, thereby taking the spotlight on SERPs. This leads to more zero-click searches, where users find the information they need without clicking through to a website. To ensure your website benefits from this new SEO trend: Concentrate on producing unique and informative content that stands out from what AI and Google already know. As Google and other search engines begin linking sources to their AI-generated summaries, having distinctive content gives you an edge. Build an enduring online brand presence and reputation. When your business is recognized and frequently searched for, it enhances your credibility and increases the likelihood of being referenced in these summaries and AI search results. Create clickworthy content. Not every topic is easily summarized. You can still attract traffic by covering topics that require depth and expertise. Or, introduce a unique angle or perspective that offers something a summary can’t. Read our beginner’s guide to AI SEO 2. AI optimization and agentic AI Traditional SEO remains essential, but AI optimization is fast emerging as its next frontier. By 2026, AI optimization—sometimes referred to as AEO, GEO, or AIO—will be as critical as SEO, driven by growing adoption of AI search and the rise of agentic AI. These are autonomous systems capable of exploring, interpreting, and acting on content without human direction. Imagine having your own AI helpers acting as digital detectives, scanning the web to identify the best businesses and content for any search; that’s agentic AI. To stay competitive for AI-first buyers, it’s important to optimize your site so these AI helpers find and recommend your site to target customers. The principles of AIO AIO builds on SEO’s foundations, but the focus is on optimizing for how AI understands and interacts with your website. The core principles include: Structured data and metadata: Use schema markup in your website code to label sections like FAQs and reviews, use defined headings, and write comprehensive SEO titles and descriptions to help AI agents interpret your site’s purpose and content. Content depth and clarity: Create content that answers questions fully, with helpful context, ensuring AI can extract why your response is unique and should be recommended. Speed and accessibility: Maintain fast page load times, mobile readiness, and clean site navigation so both users and algorithms can explore your website effortlessly. Brand building: AI engines rely on “entities” to understand what a business is, what it does, and whether it’s worth recommending in results. You can build a recognizable entity and brand by creating a social media presence, getting reviews, and listing your site in business directories. Adapting for AIO isn’t about replacing SEO; it’s about future-proofing it. As agentic AI and search systems continue to shape discovery and decision-making, the goal is to make your content instantly understandable to both humans and intelligent machines. Applying these tactics early will help you stay visible and relevant as search changes. Read more about AI-first shopping 3. Building brand reputation through user-generated content (UGC) Brand reputation influences not only traditional SEO, but also AI-driven search outcomes. As artificial intelligence technologies evolve, they’re relying on what others are saying about your brand to become more context-aware and trustworthy. User-generated content (UGC) is a powerful tool for building brand reputation. UGC includes customer reviews, testimonials, social media posts, and other content created by users about your brand. This type of content is highly valued by search engines and AI systems because it provides authentic, unbiased perspectives. To leverage UGC for SEO and AI search: Encourage customers to leave reviews on platforms like Google, Yelp, and industry-specific sites. Showcase testimonials and case studies on your website to highlight real-world success stories. Engage with users on social media and encourage them to share their experiences with your brand. Monitor and respond to UGC to show that you value customer feedback and are committed to improving. By building a strong brand reputation through UGC, you can enhance your visibility in both traditional and AI-driven search results. 4. Voice search optimization Voice search is becoming increasingly popular, with more people using devices like smart speakers and virtual assistants to find information. By 2026, voice search is expected to account for a significant portion of all searches. Optimizing for voice search requires a different approach than traditional SEO. Voice queries are often longer and more conversational, so your content should reflect this. Focus on answering questions directly and concisely, using natural language that matches how people speak. Additionally, ensure your website is mobile-friendly and loads quickly, as voice searches are often performed on mobile devices. 5. Video SEO Video content is growing in importance for SEO, as search engines are increasingly prioritizing video results. By 2026, video is expected to dominate online content, making video SEO a critical component of any digital strategy. To optimize your videos for search: Use descriptive titles and tags that include relevant keywords. Provide transcripts and captions to make your videos accessible and searchable. Host videos on platforms like YouTube and embed them on your website to increase visibility. Create engaging thumbnails and compelling descriptions to encourage clicks and views. By incorporating video SEO into your strategy, you can reach a wider audience and improve your search rankings. 6. Local SEO and hyper-local targeting Local SEO is essential for businesses that serve specific geographic areas. As AI search becomes more personalized, hyper-local targeting will become increasingly important. To optimize for local SEO: Claim and optimize your Google Business Profile listing. Ensure your NAP (name, address, phone number) information is consistent across all online directories. Encourage customers to leave reviews and respond to them promptly. Create location-specific content that addresses the needs and interests of your local audience. By focusing on local SEO, you can attract more customers in your area and improve your visibility in local search results. 7. Ethical SEO and transparency As AI and search algorithms become more sophisticated, ethical SEO practices will become increasingly important. Search engines are prioritizing websites that provide accurate, trustworthy, and transparent information. To practice ethical SEO: Avoid black-hat techniques like keyword stuffing, cloaking, and link schemes. Focus on creating high-quality content that provides value to users. Be transparent about your business practices and data collection policies. Stay updated with search engine guidelines and adapt your strategy accordingly. By adhering to ethical SEO practices, you can build trust with both users and search engines, leading to long-term success. Conclusion The landscape of SEO and AI search is rapidly evolving, with new trends emerging every year. By staying informed and adapting your strategy, you can stay ahead of the competition and achieve your digital marketing goals. Whether you’re optimizing for AI-first search, building brand reputation, or focusing on local SEO, the key is to prioritize user experience and provide valuable content. As we move into 2026, embracing these trends will be essential for success in the digital world.
